title: asystasia scandens
intro: Asystasia scandens, also known as the Creeping Foxglove or Creeping Foxglove Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and Asia, and is often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Asystasia scandens can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies, including poultices and ointments.
